title: Decoding Higher Education
text:
While university placement data is public, there is a lack of analytical perspective on how factors like faculty size and location impact a department’s popularity. This project identifies the underlying drivers of success for Industrial Engineering departments in Turkey.
Team Name: [Let It Happen]
Team Members: * Yusuf Bora Çavdar
* Onur Furkan Gök

title: Decoding Higher Education
text:
While university placement data is public, there is a lack of analytical perspective on how factors like faculty size and location impact a department’s popularity. This project identifies the underlying drivers of success for Industrial Engineering departments in Turkey.

title: Data Analytics & Results
text:
Comprehensive Data: Multi-year data for Industrial Engineering programs dynamically extracted via the thestats package.
Analytical Approach: Moving from descriptive statistics to predictive and actionable insights.
Faculty Factor: Measuring the real impact of academic staff size on student rankings.
Regional Dynamics: Identifying how geographical location influences program popularity.

title: Data Analytics & Results
text:
Exploratory Analysis
(Burada harika ggplot2 grafiklerimiz ve yorumlarımız olacak)

title: Data Analytics & Results
text:
1. Introduction to Analysis
This section presents our exploratory data analysis and findings. By leveraging the power of tidyverse and ggplot2, we aim to uncover the trends that define Industrial Engineering education in Turkey. We prioritize data-driven narrative over raw outputs.

title: Data Analytics & Results
text:
2. Preliminary Data Exploration
Instead of relying on static Excel files, we establish a direct connection to the YÖK Atlas database. Below, we extract the placement scores and university details for Industrial Engineering departments.
#| label: data-extraction #| message: false #| warning: false #| code-fold: true

title: Data Preparation
text: [cite_start]Our analysis is based on dynamic data retrieval from the YÖK Atlas system[cite: 89]. [cite_start]By using the thestats R package (developed by M. Çavuş and O. Aydın), we ensure our study is fully reproducible and updated with the latest 2018-2025 statistics[cite: 90, 91].

title: Data Preparation
text:
Data Dictionary
[cite_start]Our dataset consists of time-series information regarding Industrial Engineering programs in Turkey[cite: 91]. Below are the key variables we focus on:







Variable
Description




University_Type
[cite_start]Categorizes institutions as State or Foundation[cite: 94].


Base_Score
[cite_start]The minimum score required for student placement[cite: 91].


Success_Rank
[cite_start]The national ranking of the last student placed in the program[cite: 91].


Academic_Staff
[cite_start]Total number of professors, associate professors, and doctoral faculty[cite: 91].


Erasmus_Mobility
[cite_start]Traffic of students participating in Erasmus exchange programs[cite: 91].


City
[cite_start]The geographical location of the university[cite: 84].

title: Data Preparation
text:
Data Acquisition & Preprocessing
[cite_start]The primary goal of this phase is to transform raw data from YÖK Atlas into a tidy format suitable for analysis.

1. Data Import
[cite_start]We utilized the thestats package to query university data directly into our R environment[cite: 90].
#| label: data-import #| message: false #| code-fold: true #| code-summary: “Show code for data extraction”
library(thestats) library(tidyverse)
